requirement: the format specifies its core and how it extends and evolves

findings.observation: The minimal core is named: a required ratingScale (at least two ordered levels) plus at least one of factors/requirements/targets.
Author-side extension is defined: targets and factors/sub-factors nest to any depth, and body sections MAY be added/renamed/reordered with unrecognized body content preserved.

Reader handling of unrecognized frontmatter keys leans toward rejection ('does not conform to these shapes is not valid'; conformance must not add functionality where it causes non-conformance), so new frontmatter keys are not an articulated extension path.

No forward-versioning path is specified: the spec carries a document version ('0.1 — Draft') but defines no file schema-version, compatibility policy, or how a newer file is treated by an older reader.

rationale: The minimal core and structural (nesting/body) extension are well specified and unrecognized body content is preserved, holding the floor. But 'how it evolves' is largely absent — no file-versioning or forward-compatibility path, and unrecognized frontmatter keys are not framed as extension. Rated minimum.
